how_it_works: The Sanctum (Trial of the Sekhemas) uses Resolve as your health pool. Losing all Resolve fails the current floor. Afflictions are penalties accumulated from room chests, floor modifiers, or Corrupted Virtue. Boons are bonuses from Boon rooms, Divine Fonts, and specific rooms.
tips: Blind Devotion and Corrupted Virtue are run-killers — only accept if already stacked with Boons.
Eroding Soul reduces your maximum Resolve permanently each room — Fortified Soul (Boon) counters it.
Looming Presence stacks with other Resolve-loss sources — becomes lethal if combined with Devout Offering.
Foul Prescriptions turns all future Boons into Afflictions — essentially a Blind Devotion variant.
Heretical Rites prevents healing via Divine Font — track remaining rooms before accepting it.
Dead Weight can strip your most valuable Boon — assess which Boon you would most hate to lose.
Familiar Sting is only dangerous for non-Chaos-immune builds — less relevant with high Chaos res.

afflictions.name: Blind Devotion
afflictions.effect: Cannot gain Boons for the rest of this Sanctum run.
afflictions.notes: Effectively makes all Boon rooms worthless for the entire run. Treat as a run-ender unless you already have strong Boons.

afflictions.name: Corrupted Virtue
afflictions.effect: Gain Afflictions from Boon rooms instead of Boons.
afflictions.notes: Same impact as Blind Devotion but actively hurts you by adding Afflictions. Avoid at nearly all costs.

afflictions.name: Foul Prescriptions
afflictions.effect: All Boons you gain are Afflictions instead.
afflictions.notes: Functionally identical to Corrupted Virtue — Boon rooms become Affliction rooms. Run-ending in most cases.

afflictions.name: Eroding Soul
afflictions.effect: Lose a portion of your maximum Resolve after each room.
afflictions.notes: Maximum Resolve loss compounds over the floor. Counter with Fortified Soul (Boon) or Encouraging Devotion.

afflictions.name: Looming Presence
afflictions.effect: Resolve loss from all sources is increased.
afflictions.notes: Multiplicative with all other Resolve loss — stacks badly with Devout Offering or Eroding Soul.

afflictions.name: Devout Offering
afflictions.effect: Lose Resolve at the end of each completed room.
afflictions.notes: Consistent drain each room. More dangerous on longer floors with many rooms. Combine with Looming Presence is very bad.

afflictions.name: Dead Weight
afflictions.effect: Lose a random Boon when you are Hit.
afflictions.notes: Can strip your best Boon unexpectedly. More dangerous if you have few Boons with high variance.

afflictions.name: Distant Memory
afflictions.effect: Lose a Relic at the start of each floor.
afflictions.notes: Destroys Relics (your passive bonuses for the run). Very bad if you have high-value Relics equipped.

afflictions.name: Heretical Rites
afflictions.effect: Cannot use the Divine Font or Blacksmith's Bench.
afflictions.notes: Divine Font restores Resolve and provides Boons. Losing access removes a key recovery mechanism.

afflictions.name: Defiled Shrine
afflictions.effect: Shrines are Defiled — interacting with them deals damage and has harmful effects.
afflictions.notes: Avoid interacting with Shrines when afflicted. Some Shrines are unavoidable room paths — check layout first.

afflictions.name: Dark Pit
afflictions.effect: Lose Resolve when you land a Critical Strike.
afflictions.notes: Significantly impacts Critical Strike builds. Non-crit builds can ignore this almost entirely.

afflictions.name: Guilt-Ridden
afflictions.effect: Lose Resolve when you pick up Aureus coins.
afflictions.notes: Forces a trade-off: Aureus funds Boons and room purchases. Skip high coin clusters when low on Resolve.

afflictions.name: Dishonoured Invitation
afflictions.effect: Cannot collect Aureus coins.
afflictions.notes: Aureus is used to buy Boons and access Merchant rooms. Without it you lose access to optional upgrades.

afflictions.name: Familiar Sting
afflictions.effect: Poison does not wear off for the rest of this Sanctum run.
afflictions.notes: Stacking poison from multiple sources can deal lethal damage. Chaos resistance and flask poison removal help significantly.

afflictions.name: Lethal Presence
afflictions.effect: Enemies deal increased damage when close to you.
afflictions.notes: Disproportionately punishes melee builds. Ranged characters can maintain safe distance to minimize the effect.

afflictions.name: Expropriation
afflictions.effect: Monsters steal your Aureus coins on hit.
afflictions.notes: Combines badly with Dishonoured Invitation. Avoid taking many hits; the Aureus loss is not directly dangerous but limits your Boon economy.

afflictions.name: Fleeting Endurance
afflictions.effect: Endurance charges expire 50% faster.
afflictions.notes: Only impacts builds that rely on Endurance charges for mitigation. Most builds can ignore this.

afflictions.name: Lethal Concoctions
afflictions.effect: Monsters drop volatile flasks on death that explode for damage.
afflictions.notes: Move away from dying monsters to avoid the flask explosion. The damage is significant but avoidable.

afflictions.name: Grasping Shadows
afflictions.effect: Rare monsters pull you toward them on their attacks.
afflictions.notes: Can pull you into hazards or dense monster packs. Keep movement skills ready to escape.

afflictions.name: Living Terror
afflictions.effect: Rare and Unique monsters revive once when slain.
afflictions.notes: Effectively doubles Rare monster durability. Plan for extended fights and avoid lingering near dying Rares.

boons.name: Fortified Soul
boons.notes: Directly counters Eroding Soul. Provides a larger safety buffer for the entire floor.

boons.name: Encouraging Devotion
boons.effect: Gain more Resolve from all sources.
boons.notes: Multiplies the value of every other Resolve restoration source. Scales well with other Resolve Boons.

boons.name: Catharsis
boons.effect: Gain Resolve when you complete a room.
boons.notes: Passive Resolve recovery every room — one of the most consistently valuable Boons in the game.

boons.name: Opportune Timing
boons.effect: Gain Resolve when you kill a Rare or Unique monster.
boons.notes: Strong in floors with many Rare monsters. Rare rooms and Boss rooms provide the biggest gains.

boons.name: Invigorating Inscription
boons.effect: Gain Resolve when you complete a room without losing any Resolve.
boons.notes: Difficult to trigger consistently but very powerful when achieved. Combos well with dodge and evasion builds.

boons.name: Living by the Sword
boons.notes: Provides consistent Resolve recovery for builds with fast kill speed. Less effective in rooms with few monsters.

boons.name: Bronze Shrine
boons.effect: Non-Defiled Shrines grant 2 effects instead of 1.
boons.notes: Doubles Shrine value. Particularly strong if you have many Shrines remaining on the floor. Countered by Defiled Shrine affliction.

boons.name: Dousing Elixir
boons.effect: Automatically use a Flask when entering a new room.
boons.notes: Provides a free flask activation per room. Works especially well with Life/Hybrid flasks and Quicksilver flasks.

boons.name: Life Insurance
boons.effect: The first time you would lose Resolve in each room, restore a large amount of Resolve instead.
boons.notes: Functions as a safety net per room — the first hit doesn't cost Resolve. Very strong on rooms with dangerous opening attacks.

boons.name: Divine Guidance
boons.effect: Gain a random Relic at the start of the floor.
boons.notes: Free Relic slot. Value depends on what Relic is awarded. Counters Distant Memory affliction partially.

boons.name: Champion of the Cause
boons.effect: Consecrated Ground effects last longer and have increased area.
boons.notes: Useful for builds that use Consecrated Ground (Consecrated Path, Holy Flame Totem). Niche but free.

boons.name: Gilded Fortune
boons.effect: Gain bonus Aureus from all sources.
boons.notes: More Aureus means more Boon purchases from Merchants. The value is indirect — only strong if you have rooms to spend it in.
